page: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor runs at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz and is equipped with a 48 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU evaluations, and multi-threaded Geekbench workloads, including software compilation and ray tracing. Large-block memory read and write bandwidth are also highly rated, reaching 294.62 GB/sec and 135.63 GB/sec respectively. However, single-core performance remains average, as demonstrated by single-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench scores. Furthermore, while the instance handles smaller language model inference adequately, it exhibits weak performance in text generation for large 70B parameter models, producing only 2.61 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D64_v5 offers high resource density for multi-threaded applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage requires reliance on external network storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. Given its strong multi-core processing, high database operation scores, and high static web serving throughput, this instance is best suited for enterprise web serving, relational database operations, Redis deployments, and parallelized build systems.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and efficient database operations, though single-core execution and large-scale LLM text generation are relatively average to weak. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring external storage attachments. This hardware profile is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, including database management, Redis caching, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, designed for databases and web serving without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded database and web workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth for enterprise database and web workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Strong multi-core performance and high large-block memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
Average single-core performance and weak large-model LLM text generation
